text: Session 3 (first session with no persisted state files -- sessions 1 and 2 were both archived same-day by operator memory resets for single-stock fixation and then a repeat no-trade pattern; docs/archive/proteus_session1_2026-07-04.md and proteus_session2_2026-07-04.md). Read prereg, RESEARCH_LEDGER, and the journal engine fresh. Today is Saturday 2026-07-04 (NYSE/Nasdaq closed Jul 3 observed + weekend, reopens Mon Jul 6); all quotes below are Thursday Jul 2's last prints. Breadth-before-depth sweep: Nemesis's pipeline (SNDK/AMRZ/RESOLUTE already status=entered by Nemesis, redundant), Oracle's 40-symbol activist-13D list and insider clusters (table-stakes per standing findings), Midas's convergence dossiers (same). Then completed, for the first time across three sessions, the EQPT deepening pass queued by session 2: pulled the ACTUAL Schedule 13D primary XML (accession 0000950103-26-009395) and the voting-agreement exhibit directly from EDGAR rather than trusting secondary summaries. Two corrections to what the prior archived sessions assumed: (1) Item 4 Purpose-of-Transaction is GENERIC 13D boilerplate ('may pursue extraordinary corporate transactions... as officers and directors participate in ordinary business deliberations') -- NOT a stated go-private intent as session 1 characterized it; this removes the differentiator that made the bull case interesting. (2) The founders' voting agreement has NO lockup/standstill and is unilaterally terminable by either founder on written notice -- the '81% combined control' is more fragile than it reads. Pulled Q1 2026 financials (rev $989M miss vs $1.068B est, EPS -$0.20 miss v/s -$0.06 est, op cash flow -$200M vs -$51M prior year) and initially read that as fundamental deterioration, but the balance-sheet check that followed (net leverage actually DECREASED to 2.8x from 3.2x YoY, $1.6B total liquidity incl. $1.276B unused revolver) contradicts that -- the cash-flow swing is capex-funded fleet growth ($328M gross equipment purchases in Q1), not distress. Full daily bar history since IPO (Jan 23) shows the 13D-adjacent price action was a rally INTO the filing (an 18.40 May low to a 26.32 Jun 18 peak) that reversed hard the day after filing (Jun 24 22.30 to Jun 25 19.69, -11.7% on 3.4x normal volume, no company- specific news found) -- consistent with the market having priced in and then rejected a bigger-buyout narrative once the boilerplate language was digested, which corroborates my own skeptical read rather than being a fresh edge I found first. Could not pull the exact per-holder lockup carve-out (founders vs VC vs employee shares) from the S-1/424B4 after three targeted fetch attempts (document too large for single-pass retrieval); what's publicly known is only the generic 180-day-from-Jan-23-IPO date (~Jul 22), which is exactly the kind of well-known, already-published calendar fact the house discipline says is unlikely to be a standalone edge. Net: EQPT thesis, on both the bull (founder buyout optionality) and bear (lockup supply + cash deterioration) sides, is WEAKER after primary-source reading than the archived sessions assumed on secondary sources alone -- this is the deepening pass actually completed, not deferred a third time, and the honest answer is pass, not 'still need to look at this.' Separately, breadth sweep surfaced a second candidate: SPCX (SpaceX) joins the Nasdaq-100 effective Jul 7 2026 under a fast-track rule (Nasdaq's May 2026 methodology change allows top-40-by-market-cap new listings in after 15 trading days, no float minimum), with an estimated $4.3B mechanical Nasdaq-100 inflow plus ~$3B Russell reweight ($7.3B combined). This is a real, calendar-certain, price-insensitive forced-flow mechanic -- exactly my mandate's cleanest edge type in principle -- but SPCX is already a >$2T market cap (7.3B against 2T+ is under 0.4% of cap) that surged 19% on debut and has been covered by CNBC, Seeking Alpha, Motley Fool, and TD Securities analyst notes well before today; this is the single most-published forced-flow event of the year on the single most-published new listing of the year, the same 'everyone already has this calendar fact' problem session 1 identified on this exact stock's lockup unlock. No differentiated angle found. Decision: NO TRADE, session 3. Market closed today regardless, so no entry was executable even if a thesis had cleared the bar. Full reasoning, corrected EQPT read, and forward watchlist written to ghost_proteus_beliefs.md.

LIVE CAPITAL GRANTED (operator directive 2026-07-04, prereg amendment #3). The operator retired Midas's live sleeve and handed me its capital before my first paper trade existed: contributed $2,000, arriving as ~$9 cash plus the proceeds of Midas's final DAKT exit (queued order 6a473615, fills at the 2026-07-06 open, ~$1,970 total at Thursday's mark; exact figure recorded at the funding sweep). The $10,000 paper book is retired flat with zero trades — grading, the checkpoint clock, and every frozen criterion carry over uncontaminated to the live book. New rails: long-only at the broker (inverse ETFs express short views), no leverage, no options, real fills instead of modeled costs. This journal continues from the ghost era; the past stands. Until pending_funding clears in proteus_sleeve.json, my sessions are research-only.
